reviewNotes.note: F22 spec explicitly lists 'Enemy sounds: hit, defeat, projectile' but there is no criterion for an enemy projectile sound (e.g., playEnemyProjectile()). C-09 only covers playEnemyHit() and playEnemyDefeat(). The generator should add a projectile sound method even though no criterion requires it, or the evaluator will note the omission. Accepted because this is a single missing method in an otherwise comprehensive 30-criterion contract.

Test steps define 'called' and 'orig' in one page.evaluate() then reference them in a later evaluate(). Playwright runs each evaluate in a fresh scope, so these variables won't persist. Evaluator should adapt by using window globals (window.__jumpCalled = true) or combining into a single evaluate. Same issue affects C-14, C-17, and C-20.

Same cross-evaluate closure issue as C-07. Variables selectCalled, confirmCalled, origS, origC defined in one evaluate and read in another. Evaluator will need to use window globals or restructure into a single evaluate call.
Same cross-evaluate closure issue. lastAmbient and stopped variables won't persist across separate evaluate calls.
Same cross-evaluate closure issue. musicStarted and musicStopped variables won't persist.

Test guesses internal property names (bossMusictempo, _bossInterval, _bossTempo) which is fragile. Evaluator should verify the updateBossMusic/setBossMusicTempo method exists, accepts a health ratio argument, and does not throw — then inspect src/audio.js source code to verify the implementation actually changes timing parameters. Functional testing of tempo difference may require reading the source.

getOwnPropertyNames only finds own properties. If AudioManager is implemented as a class instance, methods will be on the prototype, not the instance. Evaluator should also check Object.getOwnPropertyNames(Object.getPrototypeOf(AudioManager)) or use a for...in loop. However, given the project uses vanilla JS with globals, an object literal pattern is most likely, making this a low risk.

The page.evaluate check 'typeof OscillatorNode !== undefined' passes in any modern browser regardless of AudioManager implementation. The real test is the grep for .mp3/.wav/.ogg and the source code inspection, which are appropriate. The evaluate step adds no value but doesn't hurt.

coverageAssessment.specCoverage: 29/30 explicit requirements from F22 are covered. Missing: enemy projectile sound. All other player sounds (6), enemy sounds (2/3), boss sounds (4), UI sounds (7), ambient (4 worlds), boss music (procedural + dynamic tempo), and volume/mute controls are addressed.
coverageAssessment.integrationCoverage: Good. C-07 (jump), C-08 (hurt), C-13 (coin), C-14 (menu), C-17 (ambient on stage enter), C-20 (boss music on boss spawn) cover key integration points between AudioManager and existing game systems.
coverageAssessment.regressionCoverage: All 9 prior sprints listed in regressionSprints. C-27 through C-30 add sprint-specific regression for stage loading (all 12 + Citadel), player controls, and JS error freedom. Comprehensive.

criteriaQuality.notes: Criteria are well-structured with clear thresholds. Each has concrete test steps using page.evaluate(). Integration criteria (C-07, C-08, C-13, C-14, C-17, C-20) use monkey-patching to verify call chains, which is a sound approach. Cross-evaluate variable scoping issues are minor and the evaluator can work around them.

outOfScopeAssessment: Appropriate. Visual effects (Sprint 11), performance optimization (Sprint 12), spatial audio, background music (non-boss), audio visualization, and save system changes for audio prefs are correctly excluded. The distinction between ambient audio (in scope) and background music (out of scope) aligns with the spec.
overallAssessment: Strong contract with 30 criteria covering the full F22 feature surface area. The one coverage gap (enemy projectile sound) is minor. Test methodology is sound despite some cross-evaluate scoping issues that the evaluator can adapt around. Regression coverage is thorough. Accepted as-is.
